subject: Public Health
title: Measuring Mental Health in Populations
description: A short lesson to help you apply Measuring Mental Health in Populations.

hook.text: You can't weigh mental health on a scale—so how do countries track it across millions of people?

buildup.text: Surveys like the PHQ-9 for depression and GAD-7 for anxiety screen populations at scale.

discovery.text: Disability-adjusted life years (DALYs) let researchers compare mental illness burden against physical diseases.

twist.text: Depression is the leading cause of disability worldwide—outranking heart disease and cancer in DALYs lost.

climax.text: Better measurement drives better policy—you can't fund what you can't measure.

punchline.text: Depression disables more people than any other condition on Earth.

quiz.question: What is the leading cause of disability worldwide?
